descriptionInUI: Runtime for the Vietnam Ops suite -- opt-in period air-war mechanics gated by the 'Vietnam Ops' campaign settings page. Inert unless a Vietnam Ops setting is on (the mission generator emits dcsRetribution.VietnamOps only then). Arc Light: a heavy-bomber (B-52) Strike walks a carpet of bombs across its target at the run-in instead of dropping a single aimpoint (tactical strikers unaffected). AAA flak gauntlet: aircraft within range and below the ceiling of an opposing AAA gun draw barrage flak that tightens against predictable run-ins and widens when jinking. Naval gunfire support: offshore gun ships shell shore targets -- a player F10 'Naval Fire Mission' menu fires on the last F10 map marker, and (optionally) ships automatically bombard the nearest in-range enemy ground target each cadence. Coastal campaigns only. (Convoy interdiction is not a plugin behaviour: it creates a real, tracked enemy convoy in the campaign force model, hunted via an Armed Recon corridor, so it needs no runtime here.) Airbase harassment: forward, opposing-occupied airfields draw sporadic standoff rocket/mortar fire near the ramp after a startup grace period; your own spawn fields are never targeted. Super Gaggle: a formation of transport helos runs supplies into a cut-off forward friendly outpost (launch -> outpost -> back, re-rolling on a cadence), which the player can fly escort for -- the Khe Sanh hilltop resupply. FAC(A): an airborne forward air controller (OV-10 Bronco) loitering over the front marks nearby enemy ground with white-phosphorus smoke so you can visually acquire the target and roll in. Snake and nape: retarded-bomb (Snakeye) releases pressed in low and fast lay a napalm fire + bite at each bomb's REAL impact point (the weapon is tracked to detonation) -- a dry pass lays nothing, a miss burns where it missed, and the wall of fire emerges from your actual ripple; real Mk-77 fire bombs are rendered by Splash Damage and excluded here. Rewards flying the CAS run in low and on the deck; symmetric. All distances/speeds are in imperial units (ft / NM / kts).

specificOptions.nameInUI: Arc Light carpet length along the run-in (ft)
Arc Light carpet width across the run-in (ft)
Arc Light per-blast power (lb TNT equivalent)
Arc Light: range from target the bomber releases the carpet (NM)
Flak gauntlet: AAA gun horizontal reach (NM)
Flak gauntlet: effective flak ceiling above ground (ft AGL)
Flak gauntlet: tightest barrage miss, predictable flight (ft)
Flak gauntlet: loosest barrage miss, jinking (ft)
Flak gauntlet: per-burst power (small = mostly visual)
Naval gunfire: gun reach for ship/target selection (NM)
Naval gunfire: shells per fire mission
Naval gunfire: salvo dispersion radius (ft)
Naval gunfire: automatic coastal bombardment
Naval gunfire: seconds between automatic fire missions
Airbase harassment: seconds between events per field (randomized)
Airbase harassment: impacts per event (barrage)
Airbase harassment: impact dispersion radius (ft)
Airbase harassment: per-impact power (small = mostly noise)
Airbase harassment: startup grace period, no fire (s)
Super Gaggle: transit speed (kts)
Super Gaggle: transit altitude (ft AGL)
Super Gaggle: suppressor transit/attack altitude (ft MSL)
FAC(A): forward-air-controller aircraft DCS type
FAC(A): range the FAC spots + marks ground (NM)
FAC(A): seconds between target marks per FAC
Snake and nape: max height above ground at release (ft AGL)
Snake and nape: minimum ground speed at release (kts)
Snake and nape: ordnance that lays fire (comma-separated weapon-name matches)
Snake and nape: per-impact explosion power (napalm's bite)
